Title: OsAAP6 functions as an important regulator of grain protein content and nutritional quality in rice

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Amino acid permease (AAP) family; APC superfamily; PF01490 consensus domain
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Map-based cloning/QTL fine mapping; overexpression; complementation; RNAi knockdown; co-segregation analysis; qRT-PCR correlation with phenotype; GUS promoter assays; amino-acid uptake assays; metabolite profiling; TEM; subcellular localization in protoplasts
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Natural variation sequencing of OsAAP6 promoter/5'-UTR/coding region in 197 rice mini-core accessions; accession number NA
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in all examined tissues; highest in endosperm at 5–12 DAF; strong in vascular tissues of hull, root, pulvinus, internode, node, seed and endosperm; mainly in root rhizodermis and phloem
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: ZS97 allele shows higher transcript levels than NYZ, especially in endosperm at 5 DAF and 5–12 DAF
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Grain protein content; grain storage proteins (glutelins, prolamins, globulins, albumins); total amino acids
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Improves cereal grain nutritional quality and dietary protein supply for humans
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positive regulator of grain protein content and nutritional quality; increases grain storage proteins and total amino acids; also increases amylose and reduces starch and gel consistency
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Field trials over 2 years; amino-acid uptake assay in sterile culture with 20-amino-acid mixture; developmental stage effects at grain filling (5–12 DAF, 10 DAF)
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S constitutive promoter for overexpression; native OsAAP6 promoter/5'-UTR from ZS97 for complementation and GUS assays
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising target to improve rice grain protein content and nutritional quality without detectable penalty on plant morphology, flowering time or grain yield; useful allele selection or promoter engineering/editing of cis-regulatory variants in low-GPC cultivars, especially indica
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Higher protein is coupled with higher amylose, lower starch content and reduced gel consistency, potentially affecting eating/cooking quality; no effect detected on morphology, flowering time or grain yield in this study
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Gene is the cloned major QTL qPC1. Natural variation mainly resides in promoter/5'-UTR rather than coding sequence; two common 5'-UTR cis-element variants are associated with GPC diversity mainly in indica cultivars. OsAAP6 enhances root uptake and redistribution of several amino acids and enlarges protein bodies. It is a transporter/regulator of allocation rather than a biosynthetic enzyme or TF.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. qPC1 was fine-mapped to a 6.7-kb interval containing only OsAAP6/Loc_Os01g65670. 2. Overexpression and complementation increased GPC, storage proteins and total amino acids, while RNAi reduced them. 3. OsAAP6 expression level positively correlated with GPC. 4. OsAAP6 is most highly expressed in developing endosperm and vascular/root tissues and localizes to the ER. 5. Promoter/5'-UTR polymorphisms, especially two common cis-regulatory variants, are associated with GPC diversity mainly in indica accessions. 6. OsAAP6 enhances root uptake and distribution of multiple amino acids and indirectly alters starch/protein gene expression and protein body size.
